title: microlicia coriacea
intro: Microlicia coriacea, also known as the leather-leafed microlicia or pitted microlicia, is a plant species in the family Melastomataceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Microlicia coriacea include its potential to treat various health conditions such as fever, pain, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Microlicia coriacea include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are typically consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
